title: 🟡 Backtracking — Active Recall
oneSentence: Reconstruct Subsets, Permutations, Combination Sum, and N-Queens from scratch.

subsets.title: Subsets
subsets.description: Given an integer array nums of unique elements, return all possible subsets (the power set). The solution must not contain duplicate subsets. Return the solution in any order.

hints.text: What needs to be added to the result at the beginning of the recursion to capture all states?
How should you iterate to pick the next element, and where should you start?
How do you choose the current element to add to your subset?
How do you recursively explore the next choices? (Think about the starting index)
How do you undo the choice to backtrack?

template: private void backtrack(int[] nums, int start, List<Integer> bag, List<List<Integer>> result) {
// STEP 1 - SAVE: Current bag state is a valid subset
// STEP 2 - LOOP: Try including each remaining item
// STEP 3 - CHOOSE: Add item to the bag
// STEP 5 - UNCHOOSE: Remove item and try skipping it

permutations.title: Permutations
permutations.description: Given an array nums of distinct integers, return all the possible permutations. You can return the answer in any order.

hints.text: What is the base case condition to check if you have formed a complete permutation?
How do you avoid reusing an element that has already been placed in the current permutation?
How do you mark the current element as chosen?
How do you recursively call the backtracking function with the updated state?
How do you un-choose the current element for the next iterations?

combinationSum.title: Combination Sum
combinationSum.description: Given an array of distinct integers candidates and a target integer target, return a list of all unique combinations of candidates where the chosen numbers sum to target. You may return the combinations in any order.

hints.text: What is the base case for an invalid combination when the target goes below 0?
What condition means you have found a valid combination?
Where should the loop start to avoid generating duplicate combinations?
How do you make the recursive call, keeping in mind that the same number can be chosen an unlimited number of times?

nQueens.title: N-Queens
nQueens.description: The n-queens puzzle is the problem of placing n queens on an n x n chessboard such that no two queens attack each other. Given an integer n, return all distinct solutions to the n-queens puzzle.

hints.text: When do you know you have successfully placed queens in all rows?
How do you iterate over all columns in the current row?
What check must pass before you can place a queen at (row, col)?
How do you place the queen on the board?
How do you move to the next row recursively?
How do you un-place the queen to backtrack?
